channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Thousands of Albanians protest a planned luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner
item.description: Albania braces for bigger protests as Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort sparks environmental outrage.
item.content:encoded: Albania braces for bigger protests as Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort sparks environmental outrage.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: Colombia's World Cup jersey becomes a political battleground ahead of runoff election
item.description: As Colombia gears up for the World Cup, the national team's iconic yellow jersey is becoming a political battleground in a bitter presidential race.
item.content:encoded: As Colombia gears up for the World Cup, the national team's iconic yellow jersey is becoming a political battleground in a bitter presidential race.
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Scientists probe how pigeons use magnetism to navigate
item.description: Homing pigeons rely on a variety of signals to navigate, including magnetism. But it hasn't been clear how they detect magnetic cues. Researchers propose the answer may be found in the birds' livers.
item.content:encoded: Homing pigeons rely on a variety of signals to navigate, including magnetism. But it hasn't been clear how they detect magnetic cues. Researchers propose the answer may be found in the birds' livers.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Does Israel still need U.S. support?
item.description: In recent years, Israel's relationship with the U.S. has changed — not for the better, some analysts say. Now, new tensions have emerged between President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.content:encoded: In recent years, Israel's relationship with the U.S. has changed — not for the better, some analysts say. Now, new tensions have emerged between President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Jill Biden on why she was 'overwhelmed' when her husband left the 2024 race
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden reflects on the end of her husband's 2024 campaign and her time in the White House with NPR's Scott Detrow, which she details in her new memoir, View From the East Wing.
item.content:encoded: Former first lady Jill Biden reflects on the end of her husband's 2024 campaign and her time in the White House with NPR's Scott Detrow, which she details in her new memoir, View From the East Wing.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Bartlam

item.title: Fighting suspicion and mistrust about Ebola
item.description: A big challenge in fighting this Ebola outbreak is the spread of rumors and falsehoods on social media. Aid workers and officials are launching efforts to combat this misinformation.
item.content:encoded: A big challenge in fighting this Ebola outbreak is the spread of rumors and falsehoods on social media. Aid workers and officials are launching efforts to combat this misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Trump's attacks on the press often focus on women reporters who challenge him
item.description: In an exchange with CNN's Kaitlin Collins, President Trump told her she had "hatred in her eyes." We look at the president's contentious relationship with the press and women reporters in particular.
item.content:encoded: In an exchange with CNN's Kaitlin Collins, President Trump told her she had "hatred in her eyes." We look at the president's contentious relationship with the press and women reporters in particular.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Republican lawmaker on why he voted to end the war in Iran
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Republican Congressman Brian Fitzpatrick about his decision to join Democrats and three other Republicans to vote to end the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Republican Congressman Brian Fitzpatrick about his decision to join Democrats and three other Republicans to vote to end the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tinbete Ermyas

item.title: The Trump administration infrequently backs down, but it did this week
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks to The Atlantic's Anne Applebaum about why the Trump administration appears to be backing off some of its actions and what that means for concerns about democratic backsliding.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks to The Atlantic's Anne Applebaum about why the Trump administration appears to be backing off some of its actions and what that means for concerns about democratic backsliding.
item.dc:creator: Linah Mohammad

item.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
item.description: The Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker was perhaps most well-known for the graphic memoir, and subsequent film, about her life during the Iranian revolution in 1979.
item.content:encoded:
The Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker was perhaps most well-known for the graphic memoir, and subsequent film, about her life during the Iranian revolution in 1979.

(Image credit: Ernesto S. Ruscio/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck
